name: fastmail-blade-mcp
title: Fastmail
description: Fastmail email operations via JMAP — search, read, send, manage, masked email, push notifications
tagline: Full Fastmail control via JMAP with masked email
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Search & read — full-text search, thread views, snippet previews
Send & reply — compose, reply, forward with identity selection
Masked email — create, list, and manage disposable aliases
Bulk operations — move, flag, or delete batches of messages
Push notifications — real-time EventSource for new mail
Write-gated — reads always work, sends require explicit opt-in

readme:
### What it does

Fastmail Blade gives your AI agent complete email management through Fastmail's native JMAP protocol. 20 tools cover the full email lifecycle: searching messages, reading threads, composing and sending, managing mailboxes, creating masked email aliases, and subscribing to real-time push notifications for new mail.

Your agent can triage an inbox, draft replies with the correct identity, generate disposable email addresses for signups, and monitor for important messages — all without opening a browser.

### Token efficiency

JMAP is already more efficient than IMAP, but raw responses still include headers, MIME structure, and metadata your agent doesn't need. Each tool includes a concise formatter that extracts only the relevant fields. The `mail_snippets` tool returns preview-length summaries for search results, and `mail_threads` groups related messages to avoid redundant context.

### Safety model

All 20 tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — search, read, list mailboxes, check identities
- **Writes** require `FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — send, reply, move, delete, flag
- **Masked email** creation also requires the write gate

Push subscriptions are read-only and always available. Your agent can watch for new mail without any write permissions.

### Masked email

Fastmail's masked email feature lets your agent create unique, disposable addresses on the fly — useful for signups, vendor communications, or any context where you want to compartmentalise email. The `masked_create` tool generates a new alias, and `masked_list` / `masked_update` manage existing ones.

tools.name: fastmail_info
tools.description: Health check — account email, capabilities, limits.

tools.name: fastmail_identities
tools.description: List sender identities (ID, name, email) — used by mail_send + mail_reply. Stable ordering by id asc.

tools.name: mail_mailboxes
tools.description: List all mailboxes — ID, name, total/unread counts, role. Stable ordering by sortOrder asc (then name, id).

tools.name: mail_read
tools.description: Read a full email by ID — headers + body (text or HTML).

tools.name: mail_search
tools.description: Search emails with rich JMAP server-side filters (from, to, subject, body, after/before, in_mailbox, has_keyword, not_keyword, limit). Emits `_meta` envelope disclosing JMAP filter set + match cardinality.

tools.name: mail_threads
tools.description: Get all messages in a thread by ID, ordered chronologically. Emits `_meta` envelope disclosing thread scope + message count.

tools.name: mail_snippets
tools.description: Search emails returning short snippets — JMAP server-side filters (from, subject, body, after/before, in_mailbox, limit). Emits `_meta` envelope disclosing filter set + match cardinality.

tools.name: mail_state
tools.description: Get the current JMAP Email state token — used for incremental change tracking via mail_changes.

tools.name: mail_changes
tools.description: Get email changes since a previous state — server-side since_state + max_changes. Emits `_meta` envelope disclosing since-state + change cardinality.

tools.name: mail_send
tools.description: Compose and send a new email. Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: mail_reply
tools.description: Reply to an email — preserves threading. Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: mail_move
tools.description: Move one or more emails to a mailbox. Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: mail_flag
tools.description: Flag or unflag emails (set/clear a keyword). Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: mail_delete
tools.description: Delete emails — to Trash by default, or permanent. Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: mail_bulk
tools.description: Bulk action across email IDs (mark_read/unread, flag/unflag, move, delete; max 50). Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: masked_list
tools.description: List masked email aliases with server-side state + for_domain + limit filters. Emits `_meta` envelope disclosing filter set + cardinality.

tools.name: masked_create
tools.description: Create a new masked email alias. Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: masked_update
tools.description: Update a masked email alias (state/description/for_domain). Write — gated by FASTMAIL_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: push_subscribe
tools.description: Subscribe to JMAP EventSource push notifications. Blocking — returns state change events or timeout.

tools.name: push_status
tools.description: Check EventSource push notification availability and connection status.

setup.help_text: Create an API token at fastmail.com → Settings → Privacy & Security → API tokens. Enable the Mail scope.
setup.blurb: Fastmail email operations via JMAP — search, read, send, manage, masked email, push notifications

scenarios.label: Triage the morning inbox
scenarios.body: Search for unread messages, read the important ones, flag items that need follow-up, and archive newsletters and notifications in bulk.

scenarios.label: Create a masked email for a new signup
scenarios.body: Generate a disposable masked email alias for a service registration, then monitor incoming messages to that alias for a verification code.

scenarios.label: Draft and send a reply with correct identity
scenarios.body: Read the original thread, check available sending identities, compose a context-aware reply using the matching identity, and send it.
